message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_27.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 27: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[27]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_27.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 27: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: These are large files. Let me read them in chunks.

content.content:
1	"""
2	Query planner agent for polaris graph.
3	
4	Uses STORM multi-perspective methodology to produce diverse sub-queries
5	across 9 research perspectives for comprehensive evidence gathering.
6	"""
7	
8	import logging
9	import os
10	from collections import Counter
11	
12	from src.polaris_graph.tracing import get_tracer
13	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import NoEndpointError, OpenRouterClient
14	from src.polaris_graph.schemas import QueryPlan, SeedQueryPlan
15	from src.polaris_graph.state import (
16	    ResearchState,
17	    QUERIES_PER_VECTOR,
18	    STORM_PERSPECTIVES,
19	    QUERIES_PER_PERSPECTIVE,
20	    PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES,
21	)
22	
23	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
24	
25	# FIX-054: Configurable planner timeout (BUG-089).
26	# T052: All 3 SeedQueryPlan attempts timed out at 120s each.
27	# require_parameters=true may route to slower providers.
28	PG_PLANNER_TIMEOUT = int(os.getenv("PG_PLANNER_TIMEOUT", "180"))
29	
30	# FIX-C9: Configurable fallback query count (LAW VI: no hardcoded counts).
31	PG_FALLBACK_QUERY_COUNT = int(os.getenv("PG_FALLBACK_QUERY_COUNT", "22"))
32	
33	_PERSPECTIVE_QUERY_TEMPLATES = {
34	    "Regulatory": "{topic} government regulations standards compliance requirements",
35	    "Economic": "{topic} cost-effectiveness economic analysis market size investment",
36	    "Industry": "{topic} industrial application case study pilot plant scale-up deployment",
37	    "Public_Health": "{topic} health impact risk assessment epidemiology safety",
38	    "Historical": "{topic} historical development evolution timeline milestones origin",
39	    "Regional": "{topic} regional differences geographic variation country comparison",
40	    "Emerging_Trends": "{topic} recent advances novel approaches 2024 2025 breakthrough innovation",
41	    "Methodological": "{topic} methodology experimental design measurement technique protocol",
42	}
43	
44	
45	def _generate_diversity_queries(
46	    query: str,
47	    underrepresented: list[str],
48	) -> list[dict]:
49	    """RC-7: Generate targeted queries for underrepresented perspectives.
50	
51	    Returns list of SubQuery-compatible dicts.
52	    """
53	    queries = []
54	    for perspective in underrepresented[:4]:  # Cap at 4 extra perspectives
55	        template = _PERSPECTIVE_QUERY_TEMPLATES.get(perspective)
56	        if template:
57	            queries.append({
58	                "query": template.format(topic=query[:100]),
59	                "intent": f"Fill gap in {perspective} perspective",
60	                "source_preference": "both",
61	                "perspective": perspective,
62	            })
63	
64	    if queries:
65	        logger.info(
66	            "[polaris graph] RC-7: Generated %d diversity queries for perspectives: %s",
67	            len(queries),
68	            [q["perspective"] for q in queries],
69	        )
70	
71	    return queries
72	
73	
74	
75	# Build perspective description block dynamically from STORM_PERSPECTIVES
76	_PERSPECTIVE_DESCRIPTIONS = {
77	    "Scientific": "mechanisms, dose-response, empirical data, laboratory findings, systematic reviews",
78	    "Regulatory": "standards, compliance, policy frameworks, government guidelines, legal requirements",
79	    "Industry": "products, specifications, market data, manufacturer testing, industry reports",
80	    "Economic": "costs, cost-benefit analysis, market analysis, economic impact, pricing data",
81	    "Public_Health": "epidemiology, health outcomes, risk assessment, population-level data",
82	    "Historical": "development timeline, evolution of knowledge, landmark studies, foundational works",
83	    "Regional": "geographic variations, local data, country-specific regulations, regional case studies",
84	    "Methodological": "measurement protocols, study design, analytical methods, quality assurance",
85	    "Emerging_Trends": "innovations, future directions, emerging technologies, recent breakthroughs",
86	}
87	
88	_PERSPECTIVE_BLOCK = "\n".join(
89	    f"- {p}: {_PERSPECTIVE_DESCRIPTIONS.get(p, 'general research')}"
90	    for p in STORM_PERSPECTIVES
91	)
92	
93	PLANNER_SYSTEM = f"""You are a SOTA research query planner using the STORM multi-perspective methodology.
94	Your job is to decompose a research question into {QUERIES_PER_VECTOR}+ diverse, specific sub-queries
95	that maximize evidence coverage across web, academic, and government sources.
96	
97	STORM METHODOLOGY — Generate {QUERIES_PER_PERSPECTIVE} queries per perspective:
98	{_PERSPECTIVE_BLOCK}
99	
100	Rules:
101	1. Generate {QUERIES_PER_VECTOR} sub-queries minimum, covering ALL {len(STORM_PERSPECTIVES)} perspectives.
102	2. Each query MUST have a "perspective" field matching one of the perspectives above.
103	3. Include queries targeting:
104	   - Systematic reviews and meta-analyses (highest quality evidence)
105	   - Government reports and regulatory documents
106	   - Peer-reviewed journal articles
107	   - Industry standards and specifications (ISO, ANSI, IEC, etc.)
108	   - Real-world case studies and field data
109	   - Recent publications (2023-2026)
110	   - Seminal/foundational works
111	4. Vary query specificity: some broad (context), some narrow (specific data points).
112	5. For 'academic' source_preference queries, use precise technical terminology.
113	6. For 'web' queries, use more natural language to capture diverse sources.
114	7. Do NOT include domain-specific jargon unrelated to the research question.
115	
116	Output format example:
117	{{"analysis": "The question requires evidence on mechanisms, regulations, and impacts", "search_strategy": "broad", "sub_queries": [{{"query": "systematic review meta-analysis [TOPIC]", "intent": "Find synthesized evidence", "source_preference": "academic", "perspective": "Scientific"}}, {{"query": "EPA WHO regulations [TOPIC] standards 2024", "intent": "Current regulatory framework", "source_preference": "web", "perspective": "Regulatory"}}], "key_concepts": ["concept A", "concept B"], "expected_source_types": ["journal", "government", "industry"], "perspective_coverage": {{"Scientific": 6, "Regulatory": 6}}}}"""
118	
119	
120	async def plan_queries(
121	    client: OpenRouterClient,
122	    state: ResearchState,
123	) -> dict:
124	    """
125	    Plan research queries using STORM multi-perspective methodology.
126	
127	    Returns state update with sub_queries, search_strategy, and perspective_distribution.
128	    """
129	    query = state["original_query"]
130	    application = state["application"]
131	    region = state["region"]
132	    iteration = state["iteration_count"]
133	
134	    gaps_context = ""
135	    if state.get("gaps"):
136	        gaps_context = (
137	            f"\n\nPrevious iteration identified these evidence gaps:\n"
138	            + "\n".join(f"- {g}" for g in state["gaps"])
139	            + "\n\nPrioritize queries that fill these gaps."
140	        )
141	
142	    # Sprint 1B: Inject LTM prior knowledge so planner targets gaps, not duplications
143	    prior_context = ""
144	    ltm_priors = state.get("memory_ltm_priors", [])
145	    if ltm_priors:
146	        prior_lines = []
147	        for p in ltm_priors[:10]:
148	            stmt = p.get("statement", "")[:120]
149	            tier = p.get("quality_tier", "")
150	            src_vec = p.get("vector_id", "")[:30]
151	            prior_lines.append(f"- [{tier}] {stmt} (from {src_vec})")
152	        prior_context = (
153	            "\n\nPRIOR KNOWLEDGE (from previous research — target gaps, not duplications):\n"
154	            + "\n".join(prior_lines)
155	            + "\n\nWe already know the above. Focus queries on uncovered aspects "
156	            "and perspectives not represented in prior knowledge."
157	        )
158	        logger.info(
159	            "[polaris graph] Sprint 1B: Injecting %d LTM priors into planner",
160	            len(ltm_priors),
161	        )
162	
163	    # G3: Inject uploaded documents as GOLD sources for targeted queries
164	    uploaded_docs_context = ""
165	    uploaded_docs = state.get("uploaded_documents", [])
166	    if uploaded_docs:
167	        doc_lines = []
168	        for doc in uploaded_docs[:10]:
169	            fname = doc.get("filename", "unknown")
170	            preview = doc.get("content_preview", "")[:200]
171	            doc_lines.append(f"- [GOLD SOURCE] {fname}: {preview}")
172	        uploaded_docs_context = (
173	            "\n\nUPLOADED CORPORATE DOCUMENTS (GOLD tier -- treat as primary evidence):\n"
174	            + "\n".join(doc_lines)
175	            + "\n\nInclude queries that specifically extract claims from these GOLD documents."
176	        )
177	        logger.info(
178	            "[polaris graph] G3: Injecting %d uploaded documents into planner",
179	            len(uploaded_docs),
180	        )
181	
182	    # A7.4: Retrieve human overrides to avoid repeating corrected mistakes
183	    override_context = ""
184	    try:
185	        from src.polaris_graph.memory.cross_vector import query_human_overrides
186	        overrides = query_human_overrides(query=query, k=10)
187	        if overrides:
188	            override_lines = []
189	            for o in overrides[:5]:
190	                ctx = o.get("context", "")[:200]
191	                otype = o.get("override_type", "unknown")
192	                override_lines.append(f"- Previous correction ({otype}): {ctx}")
193	            override_context = (
194	                "\n\nHUMAN CORRECTION HISTORY (avoid these mistakes):\n"
195	                + "\n".join(override_lines)
196	            )
197	            logger.info(
198	                "[polaris graph] A7.4: Injecting %d human overrides into planner",
199	                len(overrides),
200	            )
201	    except Exception as ho_exc:
202	        logger.debug("[polaris graph] A7.4: Override retrieval failed (non-blocking): %s", str(ho_exc)[:100])
203	
204	    # Campaign Control Center: inject research brief for domain context
205	    research_brief_context = ""
206	    brief = state.get("research_brief", "")
207	    if brief.strip():
208	        research_brief_context = (
209	            f"\n\nRESEARCH BRIEF (domain context -- use to guide query generation):\n"
210	            f"{brief[:2000]}\n"
211	        )
212	
213	    prompt = f"""Research question: {query}
214	Application domain: {application}
215	Geographic focus: {region}
216	Iteration: {iteration + 1}
217	{gaps_context}{prior_context}{uploaded_docs_context}{override_context}{research_brief_context}
218	
219	Generate a comprehensive STORM query plan with {QUERIES_PER_VECTOR}+ sub-queries
220	covering all {len(STORM_PERSPECTIVES)} perspectives ({QUERIES_PER_PERSPECTIVE} per perspective).
221	Ensure every perspective is represented for maximum evidence diversity."""
222	
223	    # Use generate_structured() — reasoning OFF for reliable JSON.
224	    # reason() + schema causes Kimi to put JSON in reasoning_content,
225	    # which breaks parsing. generate_structured() reliably produces JSON.
226	    plan = await client.generate_structured(
227	        prompt=prompt,
228	        schema=QueryPlan,
229	        system=PLANNER_SYSTEM,
230	        max_tokens=8192,
231	        timeout=PG_PLANNER_TIMEOUT,
232	    )
233	
234	    sub_queries = [sq.query for sq in plan.sub_queries]
235	
236	    # Fallback: if LLM returned 0 queries (transient API issue),
237	    # generate basic queries from the research question
238	    if not sub_queries:
239	        logger.warning(
240	            "[polaris graph] Planner returned 0 queries — using fallback"
241	        )
242	        sub_queries = _fallback_queries(query, application, region)
243	
244	    # Compute perspective distribution from plan
245	    perspective_dist = Counter(
246	        sq.perspective for sq in plan.sub_queries
247	    )
248	
249	    # Log perspective coverage
250	    covered = [p for p in STORM_PERSPECTIVES if perspective_dist.get(p, 0) > 0]
251	    missing = [p for p in STORM_PERSPECTIVES if perspective_dist.get(p, 0) == 0]
252	
253	    logger.info(
254	        "[polaris graph] STORM Planner: %d queries, strategy=%s, "
255	        "perspectives=%d/%d covered, concepts=%s",
256	        len(sub_queries),
257	        plan.search_strategy,
258	        len(covered),
259	        len(STORM_PERSPECTIVES),
260	        plan.key_concepts[:5],
261	    )
262	    if missing:
263	        logger.warning(
264	            "[polaris graph] Missing perspectives: %s",
265	            missing,
266	        )
267	
268	    tracer = get_tracer()
269	    if tracer:
270	        tracer.evidence("plan", "query_plan", len(sub_queries),
271	            search_strategy=plan.search_strategy,
272	            key_concepts=plan.key_concepts[:10],
273	            perspective_distribution=dict(perspective_dist),
274	            covered_perspectives=covered,
275	            missing_perspectives=missing,
276	            queries=[{"query": sq.query, "perspective": sq.perspective,
277	                      "intent": sq.intent, "source_preference": sq.source_preference}
278	                     for sq in plan.sub_queries])
279	
280	    # RC-7: Source diversity — add targeted queries for underrepresented perspectives
281	    if os.getenv("PG_V3_SOURCE_DIVERSITY", "0") == "1":
282	        try:
283	            from src.polaris_graph.agents.searcher import _compute_perspective_distribution
284	            current_evidence = state.get("evidence", [])
285	            if current_evidence:
286	                distribution, underrepresented = _compute_perspective_distribution(current_evidence)
287	                if underrepresented:
288	                    diversity_queries = _generate_diversity_queries(query, underrepresented)
289	                    for dq in diversity_queries:
290	                        sub_queries.append(dq["query"])
291	                    if diversity_queries:
292	                        logger.info(
293	                            "[polaris graph] RC-7: Appended %d diversity queries to fill perspective gaps",
294	                            len(diversity_queries),
295	                        )
296	        except Exception as exc:
297	            logger.warning("[polaris graph] RC-7: Diversity query generation failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
298	
299	
300	    return {
301	        "sub_queries": sub_queries,
302	        "search_strategy": plan.search_strategy,
303	        "perspective_distribution": dict(perspective_dist),
304	        "status": "searching",
305	    }
306	
307	
308	SEED_PLANNER_SYSTEM = f"""You are a SOTA research query planner using the STORM multi-perspective methodology.
309	Your job is to generate exactly {PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES} seed queries — ONE query per perspective.
310	Each query should be the single most important search for that perspective.
311	
312	STORM PERSPECTIVES (generate exactly 1 query per perspective):
313	{_PERSPECTIVE_BLOCK}
314	
315	Rules:
316	1. Generate exactly {PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES} queries — one per perspective.
317	2. Each query MUST have a "perspective" field matching one of the perspectives above.
318	3. Each query should be the most impactful, highest-value search for its perspective.
319	4. Use "source_preference" to indicate the best source type:
320	   - "academic" for Scientific, Methodological, Historical (precise terminology)
321	   - "web" for Industry, Economic, Regional, Emerging_Trends (natural language)
322	   - "both" for Regulatory, Public_Health
323	5. These are SEED queries — an agentic loop will generate follow-up queries based on results.
324	   Focus on breadth across perspectives, not depth within any single perspective.
325	
326	Output format: {{"analysis": "Brief analysis", "sub_queries": [{{"query": "...", "intent": "...", "source_preference": "academic", "perspective": "Scientific"}}, ...]}}"""
327	
328	
329	async def plan_seed_queries(
330	    client: OpenRouterClient,
331	    state: ResearchState,
332	) -> dict:
333	    """
334	    Plan seed queries for the agentic search loop.
335	
336	    Generates exactly 9 seed queries (1 per STORM perspective) instead of 50+.
337	    The agentic loop will generate follow-up queries informed by results.
338	
339	    Returns state update with sub_queries, search_strategy, and perspective_distribution.
340	    """
341	    query = state["original_query"]
342	    application = state["application"]
343	    region = state["region"]
344	
345	    # M-16: Inject learned strategies from session feedback memory
346	    strategy_block = ""
347	    best_strategies = state.get("memory_best_strategies", [])
348	    if best_strategies:
349	        strategy_lines = []
350	        for s in best_strategies[:5]:
351	            strategy_lines.append(
352	                f"- '{s.get('query_text', '')[:80]}' ({s.get('search_type', 'web')}) -> "
353	                f"{s.get('total_evidence', 0)} evidence, relevance {s.get('avg_relevance', 0):.2f}"
354	            )
355	        strategy_block = (
356	            "\n\nLEARNED STRATEGIES (from previous successful searches):\n"
357	            + "\n".join(strategy_lines)
358	            + "\n\nUse these as inspiration for effective query patterns."
359	        )
360	        logger.info(
361	            "[polaris graph] M-16: Injecting %d learned strategies into seed planner",
362	            len(best_strategies[:5]),
363	        )
364	
365	    # Sprint 1B: Inject LTM prior knowledge into seed planner
366	    prior_context = ""
367	    ltm_priors = state.get("memory_ltm_priors", [])
368	    if ltm_priors:
369	        prior_lines = []
370	        for p in ltm_priors[:10]:
371	            stmt = p.get("statement", "")[:120]
372	            tier = p.get("quality_tier", "")
373	            prior_lines.append(f"- [{tier}] {stmt}")
374	        prior_context = (
375	            "\n\nPRIOR KNOWLEDGE (from previous research — target gaps, not duplications):\n"
376	            + "\n".join(prior_lines)
377	            + "\n\nWe already know the above. Focus seed queries on uncovered aspects."
378	        )
379	        logger.info(
380	            "[polaris graph] Sprint 1B: Injecting %d LTM priors into seed planner",
381	            len(ltm_priors),
382	        )
383	
384	    # A7.4: Retrieve human overrides to avoid repeating corrected mistakes
385	    override_context = ""
386	    try:
387	        from src.polaris_graph.memory.cross_vector import query_human_overrides
388	        overrides = query_human_overrides(query=query, k=10)
389	        if overrides:
390	            override_lines = []
391	            for o in overrides[:5]:
392	                ctx = o.get("context", "")[:200]
393	                otype = o.get("override_type", "unknown")
394	                override_lines.append(f"- Previous correction ({otype}): {ctx}")
395	            override_context = (
396	                "\n\nHUMAN CORRECTION HISTORY (avoid these mistakes):\n"
397	                + "\n".join(override_lines)
398	            )
399	            logger.info(
400	                "[polaris graph] A7.4: Injecting %d human overrides into seed planner",
401	                len(overrides),
402	            )
403	    except Exception as ho_exc:
404	        logger.debug("[polaris graph] A7.4: Override retrieval failed (non-blocking): %s", str(ho_exc)[:100])
405	
406	    # Campaign Control Center: inject research brief for domain context
407	    seed_brief_context = ""
408	    seed_brief = state.get("research_brief", "")
409	    if seed_brief.strip():
410	        seed_brief_context = (
411	            f"\n\nRESEARCH BRIEF (domain context -- use to guide query generation):\n"
412	            f"{seed_brief[:2000]}\n"
413	        )
414	
415	    prompt = f"""Research question: {query}
416	Application domain: {application}
417	Geographic focus: {region}
418	{strategy_block}{prior_context}{override_context}{seed_brief_context}
419	
420	Generate exactly {PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES} seed queries — one per STORM perspective.
421	Each query should be the single most important search for its perspective.
422	These seeds will feed an agentic search loop that generates follow-up queries based on results."""
423	
424	    # FIX-059-J: 3x retry with exponential backoff for seed planner.
425	    # T058 had only 10 queries (fallback) because single LLM call failed.
426	    import asyncio as _asyncio
427	    _seed_max_retries = 3
428	    _seed_backoff = [1, 3, 9]  # seconds
429	    plan = None
430	    sub_queries = []
431	    for _attempt in range(_seed_max_retries):
432	        try:
433	            plan = await client.generate_structured(
434	                prompt=prompt,
435	                schema=SeedQueryPlan,
436	                system=SEED_PLANNER_SYSTEM,
437	                max_tokens=4096,
438	                timeout=PG_PLANNER_TIMEOUT,
439	            )
440	            sub_queries = [sq.query for sq in plan.sub_queries]
441	            if sub_queries:
442	                break  # Success
443	            logger.warning(
444	                "[polaris graph] FIX-059-J: Seed planner returned 0 queries "
445	                "(attempt %d/%d)",
446	                _attempt + 1, _seed_max_retries,
447	            )
448	        except NoEndpointError:
449	            # I-ready-019 FL-02 (#1102): a STRUCTURAL discovery 404 in the FIRST agentic seed-planner
450	            # call reproduces every attempt — retrying is futile and the generic fallback to template
451	            # seed queries silently buries it (LAW II). Fail loud instead of degrading to templates.
452	            raise
453	        except Exception as plan_exc:
454	            logger.warning(
455	                "[polaris graph] FIX-059-J: Seed planner attempt %d/%d failed: %s",
456	                _attempt + 1, _seed_max_retries, str(plan_exc)[:200],
457	            )
458	            plan = None
459	            sub_queries = []
460	        if _attempt < _seed_max_retries - 1:
461	            await _asyncio.sleep(_seed_backoff[_attempt])
462	    if not sub_queries:
463	        logger.error(
464	            "[polaris graph] FIX-059-J: Seed planner failed after %d attempts "
465	            "— using fallback queries",
466	            _seed_max_retries,
467	        )
468	
469	    # Fallback: if LLM returned 0 queries or failed entirely, generate basic
470	    # 1-per-perspective fallback so the pipeline can proceed
471	    if not sub_queries:
472	        logger.warning(
473	            "[polaris graph] Seed planner returned 0 queries — using fallback"
474	        )
475	        sub_queries = _seed_fallback_queries(query, application, region)
476	
477	    # Compute perspective distribution
478	    perspective_dist = Counter(
479	        sq.perspective for sq in (plan.sub_queries if plan else [])
480	    )
481	
482	    covered = [p for p in STORM_PERSPECTIVES if perspective_dist.get(p, 0) > 0]
483	
484	    logger.info(
485	        "[polaris graph] Seed Planner: %d queries, perspectives=%d/%d covered "
486	        "(agentic loop will expand)",
487	        len(sub_queries),
488	        len(covered),
489	        len(STORM_PERSPECTIVES),
490	    )
491	
492	    tracer = get_tracer()
493	    if tracer:
494	        tracer.evidence("plan", "seed_query_plan", len(sub_queries),
495	            search_strategy="agentic",
496	            key_concepts=[],
497	            perspective_distribution=dict(perspective_dist),
498	            covered_perspectives=covered,
499	            missing_perspectives=[p for p in STORM_PERSPECTIVES if perspective_dist.get(p, 0) == 0],
500	            retry_attempts=_attempt + 1 if plan else _seed_max_retries,
501	            queries=[{"query": sq.query, "perspective": sq.perspective,
502	                      "intent": sq.intent, "source_preference": sq.source_preference}
503	                     for sq in (plan.sub_queries if plan else [])])
504	
505	    return {
506	        "sub_queries": sub_queries,
507	        "search_strategy": "agentic",
508	        "perspective_distribution": dict(perspective_dist),
509	        "status": "searching",
510	    }
511	
512	
513	def _seed_fallback_queries(query: str, application: str, region: str) -> list[str]:
514	    """Generate fallback queries from STORM perspectives when LLM seed planner fails.
515	
516	    FIX-C9: Capped to PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES (LAW VI). Generates one per
517	    perspective, then truncates to the configured seed query count.
518	    """
519	    templates = {
520	        "Scientific": f"systematic review {application} meta-analysis",
521	        "Regulatory": f"{application} regulatory framework {region} standards",
522	        "Industry": f"{application} industry report market data specifications",
523	        "Economic": f"{application} cost-benefit analysis economic impact",
524	        "Public_Health": f"{application} health outcomes epidemiology risk assessment",
525	        "Historical": f"{application} history development timeline foundational research",
526	        "Regional": f"{application} {region} case study regional data",
527	        "Methodological": f"{application} measurement protocol analytical methods",
528	        "Emerging_Trends": f"{application} innovation emerging technology 2025 2026",
529	    }
530	    all_queries = [templates.get(p, query) for p in STORM_PERSPECTIVES]
531	    return all_queries[:PG_AGENTIC_SEED_QUERIES]
532	
533	
534	def _fallback_queries(query: str, application: str, region: str) -> list[str]:
535	    """Generate generic topic-agnostic fallback queries when LLM planner fails.
536	
537	    Uses {application}, {region}, and {query} parameters to construct
538	    diverse queries across all STORM perspectives. No domain-specific
539	    assumptions (LAW VI compliance).
540	
541	    FIX-C9: Count configurable via PG_FALLBACK_QUERY_COUNT env var.
542	    """
543	    all_queries = [
544	        # Original query (always first)
545	        query,
546	        # Scientific
547	        f"systematic review {application} meta-analysis",
548	        f"{application} mechanisms empirical evidence",
549	        f"{application} dose-response relationship studies",
550	        f"{application} laboratory findings peer-reviewed",
551	        # Regulatory
552	        f"{application} regulatory framework {region}",
553	        f"{application} compliance standards guidelines",
554	        f"{application} government policy regulations 2024 2025",
555	        # Industry
556	        f"{application} industry report market data",
557	        f"{application} product specifications testing",
558	        f"{application} manufacturer standards ISO ANSI",
559	        # Economic
560	        f"{application} cost-benefit analysis economic impact",
561	        f"{application} market size pricing data {region}",
562	        # Public Health
563	        f"{application} health outcomes epidemiology",
564	        f"{application} risk assessment population study",
565	        # Historical
566	        f"{application} history development timeline",
567	        f"{application} foundational research seminal study",
568	        # Regional
569	        f"{application} {region} case study local data",
570	        f"{application} geographic variations regional differences",
571	        # Methodological
572	        f"{application} measurement protocol study design",
573	        f"{application} quality assurance analytical methods",
574	        # Emerging Trends
575	        f"{application} innovation emerging technology 2025 2026",
576	        f"{application} future directions research trends",
577	    ]
578	    # FIX-C9: Truncate to configured count
579	    return all_queries[:PG_FALLBACK_QUERY_COUNT]
580

content.text: No flags found so far in planner.py. Now let's read source_confidence.py (smaller, 299 lines).

content.content:
1	"""
2	Source confidence rating module for polaris graph.
3	
4	Computes composite confidence scores for evidence sources using three signals:
5	1. Open PageRank API (domain authority, 0-10 normalized to 0.0-1.0)
6	2. Source type hierarchy (static scores by publication type)
7	3. Citation count (logarithmic scaling, capped at 100)
8	
9	Composite formula: 0.4 * pagerank_norm + 0.4 * type_score + 0.2 * citation_factor
10	
11	Controlled by env var PG_SOURCE_CONFIDENCE_ENABLED (default "0" - disabled).
12	PageRank API key from env var OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY.
13	"""
14	
15	import logging
16	import os
17	from typing import Any
18	from urllib.parse import urlparse
19	
20	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
21	
22	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23	# Feature gate
24	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	# Legacy module-level constant (kept for backward compatibility in tests)
26	PG_SOURCE_CONFIDENCE_ENABLED = os.getenv("PG_SOURCE_CONFIDENCE_ENABLED", "0") == "1"
27	
28	
29	def _is_enabled() -> bool:
30	    """Runtime check for source confidence feature gate.
31	
32	    FIX-RUNTIME-GATE: Module-level constants bind at import time.
33	    If .env is loaded after import, the constant is stale.
34	    Use this function instead of PG_SOURCE_CONFIDENCE_ENABLED at call sites.
35	    """
36	    return os.getenv("PG_SOURCE_CONFIDENCE_ENABLED", "0") == "1"
37	
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	# Open PageRank API configuration
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	PAGERANK_API_URL = "https://openpagerank.com/api/v1.0/getPageRank"
42	PAGERANK_MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 100
43	PAGERANK_MAX_SCORE = 10.0
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Composite score weights
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	WEIGHT_PAGERANK = 0.4
49	WEIGHT_TYPE = 0.4
50	WEIGHT_CITATION = 0.2
51	CITATION_CAP = 100
52	
53	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# Source type hierarchy (static scores)
55	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	_SOURCE_TYPE_SCORES: dict[str, float] = {
57	    "journal_article": 0.95,
58	    "government_report": 0.90,
59	    "standard": 0.90,
60	    "book": 0.85,
61	    "industry_report": 0.70,
62	    "news": 0.60,
63	    "blog": 0.40,
64	    "unknown": 0.30,
65	}
66	
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	# Module-level domain -> pagerank cache (avoids duplicate API lookups)
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	_pagerank_cache: dict[str, float] = {}
71	
72	
73	def _extract_domain(url: str) -> str:
74	    """Extract the root domain from a URL.
75	
76	    Strips 'www.' prefix to normalize domains.
77	    Returns empty string if the URL cannot be parsed.
78	    """
79	    try:
80	        parsed = urlparse(url)
81	        hostname = parsed.hostname or ""
82	        if hostname.startswith("www."):
83	            hostname = hostname[4:]
84	        return hostname.lower()
85	    except Exception:
86	        logger.debug("Failed to parse URL for domain extraction: %s", url)
87	        return ""
88	
89	
90	def get_type_confidence(source_type: str) -> float:
91	    """Return the static confidence score for a source type.
92	
93	    Args:
94	        source_type: One of the keys in the source type hierarchy
95	                     (e.g. "journal_article", "news", "blog").
96	                     Case-insensitive; unknown types return 0.30.
97	
98	    Returns:
99	        Confidence score between 0.0 and 1.0.
100	    """
101	    normalized = source_type.strip().lower().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_")
102	    score = _SOURCE_TYPE_SCORES.get(normalized, _SOURCE_TYPE_SCORES["unknown"])
103	    return score
104	
105	
106	def compute_composite_confidence(
107	    pagerank: float,
108	    type_score: float,
109	    citation_count: int = 0,
110	) -> float:
111	    """Compute the composite confidence score from three signals.
112	
113	    Formula: 0.4 * pagerank_norm + 0.4 * type_score + 0.2 * min(citation_count/100, 1.0)
114	
115	    Args:
116	        pagerank: Normalized PageRank score (0.0-1.0).
117	        type_score: Source type confidence score (0.0-1.0).
118	        citation_count: Number of citations for the source (>= 0).
119	
120	    Returns:
121	        Composite confidence score between 0.0 and 1.0.
122	    """
123	    pagerank_clamped = max(0.0, min(1.0, pagerank))
124	    type_clamped = max(0.0, min(1.0, type_score))
125	    citation_factor = min(max(0, citation_count) / CITATION_CAP, 1.0)
126	
127	    composite = (
128	        WEIGHT_PAGERANK * pagerank_clamped
129	        + WEIGHT_TYPE * type_clamped
130	        + WEIGHT_CITATION * citation_factor
131	    )
132	    return round(composite, 4)
133	
134	
135	async def get_source_confidence(urls: list[str]) -> dict[str, float]:
136	    """Batch-lookup PageRank scores for a list of URLs.
137	
138	    Extracts the domain from each URL, deduplicates, checks the module-level
139	    cache, and queries the Open PageRank API for any uncached domains (batched
140	    up to 100 per API call).
141	
142	    Args:
143	        urls: List of source URLs to score.
144	
145	    Returns:
146	        Dict mapping each input URL to its normalized PageRank score (0.0-1.0).
147	        URLs whose domains fail lookup receive a score of 0.0.
148	    """
149	    import aiohttp
150	
151	    api_key = os.getenv("OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY", "")
152	    if not api_key:
153	        logger.warning(
154	            "[polaris graph] OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY not set; "
155	            "returning 0.0 for all URLs"
156	        )
157	        return {url: 0.0 for url in urls}
158	
159	    # Map URL -> domain, track unique domains needing lookup
160	    url_to_domain: dict[str, str] = {}
161	    domains_to_lookup: list[str] = []
162	
163	    for url in urls:
164	        domain = _extract_domain(url)
165	        url_to_domain[url] = domain
166	        if not domain:
167	            continue
168	        if domain not in _pagerank_cache and domain not in domains_to_lookup:
169	            domains_to_lookup.append(domain)
170	
171	    # Query the API in batches of PAGERANK_MAX_BATCH_SIZE
172	    if domains_to_lookup:
173	        headers = {"API-OPR": api_key}
174	        timeout = aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=30)
175	
176	        try:
177	            async with aiohttp.ClientSession(timeout=timeout) as session:
178	                for batch_start in range(
179	                    0, len(domains_to_lookup), PAGERANK_MAX_BATCH_SIZE
180	                ):
181	                    batch = domains_to_lookup[
182	                        batch_start : batch_start + PAGERANK_MAX_BATCH_SIZE
183	                    ]
184	                    params: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
185	                        ("domains[]", domain) for domain in batch
186	                    ]
187	
188	                    try:
189	                        async with session.get(
190	                            PAGERANK_API_URL,
191	                            headers=headers,
192	                            params=params,
193	                        ) as response:
194	                            if response.status != 200:
195	                                logger.warning(
196	                                    "[polaris graph] PageRank API returned "
197	                                    "status %d for batch of %d domains",
198	                                    response.status,
199	                                    len(batch),
200	                                )
201	                                # Cache failures as 0.0 so we don't retry
202	                                for domain in batch:
203	                                    _pagerank_cache.setdefault(domain, 0.0)
204	                                continue
205	
206	                            data: dict[str, Any] = await response.json()
207	                            _parse_pagerank_response(data, batch)
208	
209	                    except aiohttp.ClientError as exc:
210	                        logger.warning(
211	                            "[polaris graph] PageRank API network error "
212	                            "for batch of %d domains: %s",
213	                            len(batch),
214	                            exc,
215	                        )
216	                        for domain in batch:
217	                            _pagerank_cache.setdefault(domain, 0.0)
218	
219	        except Exception as exc:
220	            logger.error(
221	                "[polaris graph] PageRank API session error: %s", exc
222	            )
223	            for domain in domains_to_lookup:
224	                _pagerank_cache.setdefault(domain, 0.0)
225	
226	    # Build result mapping
227	    result: dict[str, float] = {}
228	    for url in urls:
229	        domain = url_to_domain.get(url, "")
230	        raw_score = _pagerank_cache.get(domain, 0.0)
231	        result[url] = round(raw_score / PAGERANK_MAX_SCORE, 4)
232	
233	    logger.info(
234	        "[polaris graph] PageRank lookup: %d URLs, %d unique domains, "
235	        "%d cache hits, %d API lookups",
236	        len(urls),
237	        len(set(url_to_domain.values()) - {""}),
238	        len(set(url_to_domain.values()) - {""}) - len(domains_to_lookup),
239	        len(domains_to_lookup),
240	    )
241	
242	    return result
243	
244	
245	def _parse_pagerank_response(
246	    data: dict[str, Any], batch: list[str]
247	) -> None:
248	    """Parse the Open PageRank API response and populate the cache.
249	
250	    The API returns a response shaped like:
251	    {
252	        "status_code": 200,
253	        "response": [
254	            {
255	                "status_code": 200,
256	                "domain": "example.com",
257	                "page_rank_integer": 7,
258	                "page_rank_decimal": 7.23,
259	                "rank": "12345"
260	            },
261	            ...
262	        ]
263	    }
264	
265	    Args:
266	        data: Parsed JSON response from the API.
267	        batch: List of domains in this batch (used for fallback on missing).
268	    """
269	    seen_domains: set[str] = set()
270	    response_items = data.get("response", [])
271	
272	    for item in response_items:
273	        if not isinstance(item, dict):
274	            continue
275	
276	        domain = item.get("domain", "")
277	        if not domain:
278	            continue
279	
280	        seen_domains.add(domain.lower())
281	
282	        # Prefer the decimal score for precision
283	        score = item.get("page_rank_decimal")
284	        if score is None:
285	            score = item.get("page_rank_integer", 0)
286	
287	        try:
288	            score = float(score)
289	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
290	            score = 0.0
291	
292	        # Clamp to valid range
293	        score = max(0.0, min(PAGERANK_MAX_SCORE, score))
294	        _pagerank_cache[domain.lower()] = score
295	
296	    # Any domains not in the response get cached as 0.0
297	    for domain in batch:
298	        if domain.lower() not in seen_domains:
299	            _pagerank_cache.setdefault(domain.lower(), 0.0)
300
